expect: maintenance.py --quick stops surfacing trimmed L-1489 and task_order no longer ranks the stale DUE item first
actual: Confirmed. Marked `check_lessons` as a live check in `tools/maintenance.py`, so `maintenance.py --quick` stopped replaying the stale `L-1489` over-20-lines alert. `workspace/maintenance-actions.json` now exports the real current DUE set, and `pwsh -NoProfile -File tools/task_order.ps1` no longer ranks the stale trim debt.
diff: Expected the dead `L-1489` DUE to disappear from maintenance/task-order surfacing. It did. Secondary finding: once the false positive cleared, maintenance immediately exposed live debt instead of an empty queue, so the cache bug was masking current coordination/state work rather than inventing a harmless extra warning.
